elements.name: Title
texts: An omen of disaster prior to the 1795 accident

elements.name: Description
texts: "As a boat was being loaded with passengers at eleven o'clock, an elderly man arrived at the shore and urgently signalled to his son to come away with him..."

elements.name: Source
texts: Curtayne, Alice, Lough Derg: St. Patrick’s Purgatory, p. 105

elements.name: Publisher
texts: Burns Oats and Washbourn, Ltd., London and Dublin

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "As a boat was being loaded with passengers at eleven o'clock, an elderly man arrived at the shore and urgently signaled to his son to come away with him. The young man protested: he was fasting; he was intent on making the pilgrimage. But the father insisted strongly that he had an extremely alarming dream and that his son would have to postpone the pilgrimage, at least for a day. Both left together."
